title: KIND support for podman machine
body:
/kind "podman machine"

**Description**

I'm very close to completely replacing Docker desktop with `podman machine` on MacOS however, one aspect I use frequently is Kubernetes in Docker (KIND) which so far I've been unsuccessful.  [KIND documentation](https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/rootless/#host-requirements) shows how the podman host should be configured for KIND to work in a rootless environment.  I'm hoping these configurations can be applied to the QEMU guest as I don't believe `podman machine` currently allows custom configuration.


**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. [Install KIND](https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/quick-start/#installation)

2. Run KIND [rootless podman option](https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/rootless/#creating-a-kind-cluster-with-rootless-podman): KIND_EXPERIMENTAL_PROVIDER=podman kind create cluster


**Describe the results you received:**
KIND begins to build a cluster then provides the following error:
_Command Output: Error: error configuring network namespace for container 24af67548b7065324c115b1f6e2777765d2c0da01184bf9733873a54a662359e: error adding pod kind-control-plane_kind-control-plane to CNI network "kind": failed to list chains: running [/usr/sbin/ip6tables -t nat -S --wait]: exit status 3: modprobe: ERROR: could not insert 'ip6_tables': Operation not permitted
ip6tables v1.8.7 (legacy): can't initialize ip6tables table `nat': Table does not exist (do you need to insmod?)
Perhaps ip6tables or your kernel needs to be upgraded._

**Describe the results you expected:**
Successful provisioning of KIND cluster

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**
KIND provides [recommended host configurations](https://kind.sigs.k8s.io/docs/user/rootless/#host-requirements) related to IPv6 iptables which appears to not be compatible with the current QEMU `podman machine` host.

**Output of `podman version`:**


```
$ podman version

Version:      3.4.0

```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**

comments.body:
Not sure why this is a Podman issue versus a KIND issue.

Does kind require ipv6 and podman manchine/fedora coreos don't have ipv6 enabled by default?

@rhatdan agreed, not a podman specific issue.  I was hoping for the QEMU host to be configured according to KIND doc suggestions or have the ability to manage those aspects myself, similar to what is available for [LIMA QEMU hosts](https://github.com/lima-vm/lima#can-i-run-other-container-engines-such-as-docker-and-podman).  I'm thinking this is mostly a configuration aspect for the `podman machine` VM?  I'd value this capability as a podman user on a Mac but would understand if this request was not chosen to complete.  There are other tooling options for me to replace Docker desktop with.

You can use `podman machine ssh` to enter the VM. There you can apply all the configuration you want.
I don't think podman should apply such configuration by default.
